name: Spanish A2 Vocabulary
description: Elementary Spanish. Past tense, reflexive verbs, daily life vocabulary. Can handle most tourist situations.

facts.explanation: abertura — opening (act of opening). Also: opening (something open), aperture
abrazar — to hug, to embrace (a person, an animal). Also: to embrace; to adopt (a religion or way of life), to hug, to embrace, to cuddle, to hold (each other, one another)
abuela — grandmother, female equivalent of abuelo. Also: old woman
academia — academy.
acceso — access. Also: sexual intercourse, passage, attack; fit
acción — action, act, deed (something done). Also: action (way of motion or functioning), action (fast-paced activity), combat, action, general legal action; lawsuit, share (financial instrument)
aceituna — olive (fruit).
acento — accent (typographical line). Also: accent (variety of speech connected to a certain geographical region)
aceptar — to accept, to take up, to take up on. Also: to agree, to come to terms with
acogida — welcome, welcoming. Also: reception
aconsejable — advisable.
acontecimiento — event, development, occurrence, happening.
acostumbrar — accustom, inure, familiarize. Also: to use to (be in the habit of), to get accustomed to
adaptar — to adapt, to adjust, to tailor (to make suitable). Also: to adapt (to fit by alteration), to adapt (to change so as to be adapted), to suit, to fit
adecuar — to adapt, adjust.
adelgazar — to make thin or slender. Also: to refine, purify, to lose weight, to become thin or slender; to lose weight
adivinar — to guess. Also: to divine, to foretell, to discern
administrar — to administer (cause to take). Also: to administer, to manage (manage or supervise the conduct of)
adorar — to adore (both literally and in the context of love). Also: to worship

afectar — to affect, to have an effect on, to influence. Also: to feign, to affect, to speak or act insincerely, adopt an affectation, to impair, to affect (negatively), to get to, to get under one's skin, to upset, to affect emotionally, to be affected (by an action), to get to
afectivo — affective. Also: emotional
afeitar — to shave (to remove hair with a razor or clippers). Also: to groom
afición — fondness, inclination. Also: hobby, persistence, determination, zeal
afirmación — claim or statement of something believed to be true.
africano — African (of, from or relating to Africa).
afueras — outskirts, suburbs.
agenda — agenda, planner (notebook). Also: agenda (list of matters to be taken up)

agitar — to shake, to agitate, to churn, to churn up. Also: to wave, to flail, to wag, to stir, to stir up, to agitate, to shake up, to rattle, to ruffle (provoke), to flap (e.g., wings, sails), to churn, to be shaken, to be tossed (about), to be stirred, to be agitated, to flail, shake (to wave or swing vigorously)
agotar — to exhaust, deplete, use up. Also: to run out, to sell out, to wear oneself out
agradecer — to thank. Also: to be grateful for
agricultura — agriculture, farming.
agrícola — agricultural.
aguardiente — liquor / hard liquor, booze. Also: brandy
ahorro — saving (money that is saved).
aimara — Aymara.
aislar — to isolate, to insulate, to sequester, to separate, to quarantine. Also: to insulate (surround with protecting material to prevent transfer of heat, electricity, etc.)
ajedrez — chess.
alarma — alarm (a summons to arms). Also: alarm (a sound intended to give notice of approaching danger), alarm (a mechanical device for rousing attention), alarm (sudden surprise with fear of danger)

alcalde — mayor (the leader of a city or a municipality). Also: an official such as an administrator, mayor, or judge, A variation of brisca for three players, that is played with the forty-card pack of Spanish-suited playing cards.
alcohólico — alcoholic.
alegrar — to make happy. Also: to be happy, to be glad
alegría — joy, happiness. Also: Any of various amaranths
algodón — cotton (plant, textile fiber). Also: cotton wool, ellipsis of algodón de azúcar
alianza — alliance. Also: wedding ring, wedding band, political coalition, covenant, ark
alimentar — to feed (give food to eat). Also: to feed into, reinforce.
alma — soul. Also: web (strip of material between flanges)

almacén — warehouse. Also: shop, store; grocer's, storeroom, A store selling domestic goods, either retail or wholesale., A store selling household electronics., A store selling textiles and possibly clothing.
alpinismo — mountaineering, alpinism.
altiplano — a high plateau.
altitud — altitude. Also: height
amazónico — Amazonian.
ambicioso — ambitious (possessing, or controlled by, ambition).
amueblar — to furnish.
andaluz — Andalusian (of, from or relating to Andalusia, Spain).
andino — Andean.
anfiteatro — amphitheater.
anoche — last night.
anotar — to annotate. Also: to score, to note
anterior — anterior (in time), previous, earlier, former, past. Also: anterior (in space)
antigüedad — antiquity. Also: antique (an old object perceived as having value because of its aesthetic or historical significance), tenure, seniority
anunciar — to announce, to herald, to proclaim. Also: to annunciate, to advertise, to bode, approach, impend, be imminent (indicate by signs, as future events), to announce oneself

apagar — to extinguish (a flame, fire), to douse. Also: to satisfy, quench, slake (thirst, etc.), to shut off, to turn off, to switch off, to shut down, to power off, to put out, to stub out (e.g., cigarette), to blow out (e.g., a candle), to snuff or snuff out (e.g., a torch), to fade; to wane, to go out, be extinguished
aparato — apparatus, device, tool, instrument, appliance. Also: blower, piece (phone), braces (a device worn on the teeth to straighten them), willy, tool (, pomp, ceremony
aparcamiento — parking. Also: parking garage, parking lot, car park
aparcar — to park (to bring a vehicle to a halt in a specified place).
aparecer — to appear, to turn up, to pop up, to come up.
aperitivo — apéritif. Also: appetizer, snack
apertura — opening (act of making something open). Also: aperture (something which restricts the diameter of a light path), opening (the first few moves), opening ceremony, openness, open-mindedness
apetecer — to feel like, to fancy; would like, to be down for (US slang).
aplaudir — to applaud.

aplicar — to use, to apply. Also: to smear, to put something above another thing or in contact with another thing, to apply (to submit oneself as a candidate), to apply oneself, to apply, to hold true
apreciar — to appreciate (to view as valuable). Also: to appreciate (to increase in value), to notice, to detect
aprendizaje — learning. Also: apprenticeship

apretar — to squeeze. Also: to tighten, constrict or compress, to press (e.g., a button), to pull (e.g., the trigger), to grit (e.g., one's teeth), to clench (e.g., one's fist), to push down (e.g., a toilet plunger), to hold back, to hold on to something, to start an action
aprobar — to approve, to condone, to permit. Also: to pass (succeed, e.g. on an exam)
archivo — archive. Also: file, filing cabinet
armario — cupboard, storage cabinet, wardrobe. Also: wardrobe, closet (movable furniture for storing clothes), locker
aromático — aromatic (fragrant). Also: aromatic

arreglar — to fix, to repair, to mend. Also: to settle, to sort, to sort out, to patch up, to solve, to straighten out, to fix up, to tidy, to tidy up, to clean up, to arrange, to adjust, to get ready, to get by, to make do, to manage, to work out, to straighten out
arriba — above, over. Also: up; uphill; upstream; upstairs
artesanía — craft, handicraft, handcraft, craftwork.
ascensor — elevator, lift.
asistente — attendee. Also: assistant
asistir — to attend (an event). Also: to assist
asombrar — to amaze. Also: to astonish, to astound
aspirina — aspirin.
asustar — to scare, to frighten, to spook. Also: to freak out (somebody), to creep out, to startle, to be scared, frightened, to freak out, to get freaked out, to panic

ataque — attack (an attempt to damage an opponent or enemy). Also: attack, offensive, attack (the sudden onset of a disease or condition), attack (an attempt to detract from the worth or credibility of someone), onset of a syllable
atasco — jam, blockage. Also: obstruction
atención — attention. Also: courtesy, favor
aterrizar — to land (e.g. an airplane). Also: to come back to reality, to stop dreaming
atrás — behind. Also: back in time, ago
aumentar — to increase, grow. Also: to increase, augment, to enhance, to accumulate, to increase, to augment
aun — even (implying an extreme example in the case mentioned). Also: still, yet
automático — automatic.
auténtico — authentic, real, true (conforming to reality and therefore worthy of trust).
autóctono — native, autochthon.
auxiliar — auxiliary, ancillary. Also: subsidiary, support (e.g. support staff)
aventurero — adventurous, adventuresome.
avisar — to tell, tip off, notify, inform. Also: to warn, to ask for the services of (a professional)
aviso — warning. Also: announcement
ayer — yesterday. Also: days gone by; yesteryear; the past
ayuntamiento — city council, town council, council (a corporation or body of magistrates in cities and towns, corresponding to mayor and aldermen). Also: city hall, town hall, sexual intercourse

azafata — stewardess, flight attendant. Also: a person who provides assistance to attendees, customers, clients, etc.; a conference assistant, a customer service assistant, an events coordinator, a hostess, a receptionist, etc.
balneario — seaside resort, bathing resort. Also: spa
baloncesto — basketball (sport).

barra — bar, rod (a solid, more or less rigid object of metal or other material with a uniform cross-section smaller than its length). Also: bar (a counter, or simply a cabinet, from which alcoholic drinks are served in a private house or a hotel room.), bar (various lines used as punctuation or diacritics, such as the pipe ⟨|⟩, fraction bar (as in ¹⁄₂)), slash ("/" symbol), backslash ("\" symbol), bend sinister, barbell, fan, crowd, supporter, especially political, ellipsis of barra de pan
barro — mud. Also: clay
basar — to base on.
bastar — to be enough; to suffice.
basura — trash, waste, garbage, rubbish, nonsense. Also: something trashy or undesirable
batalla — battle.
batir — to beat, to pound repeatedly. Also: to beat, whisk, whip, to beat, overcome (an opponent, record, etc.), to coin (a word, etc.)
be — The name of the Latin script letter B/b..
beca — grant, stipend. Also: scholarship
belleza — beauty (the quality of being beautiful). Also: beauty (beautiful person or object)
besar — to kiss. Also: to kiss (each other, one another), to make out with, to neck with
biografía — biography. Also: life story, bio
bloque — bloc. Also: block (street block or a piece of something), slab
blusa — blouse.
boda — wedding.
boliviano — Bolivian.
bolso — purse. Also: bag, handbag, pouch, sac, tote
bombero — firefighter.

borde — edge, border, brink, verge, rim, margin. Also: brim, rim, lip (top edge of a vessel or container), side (of the road, highway, freeway, etc.), ledge (of a window), edging, fringe (shaping or dressing the edge of something)
botón — button (clothing, mechanical). Also: key (on a musical instrument), bud (newly formed leaf or flower that has not yet unfolded), button (blunted tip of a foil)
brillante — bright, brilliant, lustrous, shiny, glowing, sparkly, glittering, glossy. Also: excellent, outstanding, exemplary, scintillating
broma — joke, prank, practical joke. Also: banter (plural), shipworm

caber — to fit, enter (to be able to be contained (by something; regardless of whether space or volume remains)). Also: to traverse, pass through or across (a way, path, door, hole, opening, mouth, orifice, etc.), to be held or contained (to be held inside (something) or passed through (regardless of whether space or volume remains)), to have, hold, should be (in certain phrases), to be acceptable, accepted, permitted, permissible, allowable, etc., to take, to understand, to be possible
cable — cable, cord. Also: wire, hand, help
cabra — female goat. Also: goat (unknown gender), girl
caja — box, case. Also: bank, cash desk, cash register, register, cash box, gearbox, snare drum, case
calamar — squid. Also: calamari
calendario — calendar.
calentar — to heat, to warm up (cause an increase in temperature). Also: to turn on (arouse sexually), to warm up (prepare for a physical activity)
calentito — churro (fried pastry).
calma — calm, stillness, peacefulness. Also: tranquility, peace and quiet

calmar — to calm, to still (to limit and lessen in motion and disturbance). Also: to calm, soothe, assuage, to ease, to appease, to quell, to allay, to settle, to relieve (to make lessen in intensity), to defuse (e.g. a situation or problem, tension or conflict, a crisis), to quench, to quell (one's thirst), to cool, to talk down, to talk off the ledge, to subside, abate, to steady (often used when talking to an animal one is riding like a horse), to calm down, to settle down, to quiet down, to calm oneself, to cool off, to cool it, to chill, to chill out, to subside
camello — camel. Also: job, work, labour, toil, pusher, drug dealer
campamento — camping (recreational activity). Also: campsite, campground, camp
campaña — campaign. Also: countryside, base
campeonato — championship.
campesino — farmer. Also: peasant
canadiense — Canadian (native or inhabitant of Canada).
canela — cinnamon. Also: something excellent, top-notch, or of the finest quality
canoa — canoe. Also: hollow hold (exercise)
cantidad — amount. Also: quantity, figure, plenty; shedload

capa — cloak, cape (a sleeveless garment hanging from the neck). Also: coat, sheet (a covering of material, such as paint), layer (a single thickness of some material covering a surface), guise; pretext
caribeño — Caribbean.
cariño — affection, fondness. Also: caress, dear, darling, honey, love, gift, wistfulness
cariñoso — loving, kind, caring, warm-hearted. Also: tender, fond, affectionate, warm, cuddly, doting
carné — identity card. Also: driver's licence

carpeta — folder (an organizer that papers are kept in). Also: folder, directory (virtual container in a computer's file system, in which files and other folders may be stored), lace doily, billiard cloth, gambling table cloth, people skills, hotel reception desk, carpet
cartel — poster, placard, bill, banner. Also: lineup, billing, the "now-showing" board in a cinema or playhouse
cartón — cardboard, paperboard. Also: cardboard box, certificate (A document serving as evidence that a person has completed an educational course)
casero — domestic, household. Also: homely, homemade
casete — cassette.

castellano — Castilian (of, from or relating to the geographic region or former kingdom of Castile, now approximately divided into the autonomous communities of Castile and León, Castilla-La Mancha and the Community of Madrid). Also: Spanish (of or related to Spain, particularly the Castilian or European forms of the Spanish language), Of a hue of marbleized black and white.
catedrático — professor.
categoría — category. Also: standing, regard
cazar — to hunt. Also: to catch (a ball, said especially of the goalkeeper)
cañaveral — reed field. Also: canebrake, sugar-cane plantation
celebración — celebration.
celular — cellular.
cementerio — cemetery. Also: junkyard, scrapyard
cepillo — brush. Also: plane (tool for smoothing wood; for carpentry), poor box
cercano — near, nearby, close.
cerebro — brain. Also: nerd
cerámica — ceramic (material). Also: ceramics (art of making ceramic objects), ceramics (ceramic objects)
cesta — basket. Also: laundry basket, hamper (made of plastic), laundry basket, hamper (made of soft material such as polyester and/or cotton)
champú — shampoo.
cheque — cheque, blank cheque. Also: checkmark, check valve
chicha — meat; flesh (culinary term). Also: boob (female breast)

chino — gobbledygook (an incomprehensible language). Also: a Chinese-run establishment, a Chinese restaurant, (US) variety store, dollar store, dime store, five-and-dime, (UK) pound shop (owned by a Chinese immigrant), a small or medium-sized supermarket owned by a Chinese immigrant, typically found in residential areas, heroin (taken by chasing the dragon)
chirimoya — the fruit of the chirimoyo, the cherimoya.
choclo — corncob. Also: corn (cereal plant)
ciento — hundred (100 units of something). Also: hundreds (an indefinite number consisting of several hundred)
científico — scientific.
cinta — ribbon, worn by girls in the hair. Also: tape, ellipsis of cinta de correr or cinta para correr; treadmill, ellipsis of cinta cinematográfica; film
circular — to circulate. Also: to go round, move around, to scram, clear off
clave — key (to a problem or puzzle). Also: password, code, clef, clave
clic — click.
coco — coconut. Also: testicle, US dollars
coincidir — to coincide. Also: to agree
colaboración — collaboration.
colaborar — to collaborate.
collar — necklace. Also: an animal's collar, band or chain around its neck
colorar — to dye, tint. Also: to color
columna — a column; a circular support. Also: file
combatir — to combat or fight.
comedia — comedy. Also: comedy (entertainment), soap opera
comedor — dining room. Also: eatery, cafeteria, canteen (staff restaurant)
comerciante — trader. Also: shopkeeper
comienzo — beginning.
comodidad — commodity, goods. Also: comfort, convenience
comparar — to compare.
completar — to complete.
complicar — to complicate. Also: to get complicated, to get messy, to make it harder or more difficult for oneself
comportamiento — behavior.
compositor — composer.

comprobar — to check, to check on, to check out, to see, to check to see (examine). Also: to verify, to ascertain, to determine, to confirm, to test (the accuracy or effectiveness of something), to prove, to establish (i.e. demonstrate the veracity of something), to note (as in realizing), to be checked, tested, to check out, to be found, determined
compromiso — commitment. Also: engagement, appointment, compromise, predicament (an unfortunate or trying position)
compás — pair of compasses (tool used to draw circles). Also: rhythm, beat, bar, time, time signature
comunitario — community.
concepto — concept. Also: conceit
condición — condition. Also: possibility; the makings
conducir — to drive; to conduct.
conductor — driver. Also: motorist, presenter; host (of a television show)
conexión — connection (the point at which two or more things are connected). Also: connection (a feeling of understanding between people)
confesar — to confess, to come clean about. Also: to confess, to come clean, to hear confession
confirmar — to confirm.
congelador — freezer.
conquista — conquest.
conquistar — to conquer. Also: to enamor, romantically convince, to charm (an object to a person)
consulado — consulate.
consulta — consultation. Also: query, question, surgery (doctor's consulting room)
consultar — to consult.
consumidor — that consumes.
consumo — consumption (the act of eating, drinking or using).
contagiar — to infect.
contaminar — to contaminate. Also: to corrupt, to profane
contemplar — to contemplate. Also: to behold, to regard, to envisage, to foresee, anticipate, prevent
contemporáneo — contemporaneous.
contener — to contain. Also: to hold, to restrain, check, to control
contenido — content (of a text, artwork or media production). Also: contents (that which is inside a container)
contentar — to please. Also: to make do, settle
contexto — context.
contrario — contrary, opposite. Also: opposed, against
contratar — to contract, to take on. Also: to hire

controlar — to control, to rein in. Also: to manage, to handle (e.g. managing symptoms, pain, stress or emotions), to monitor, to check, to supervise, to oversee, to verify, to regulate, to check on, to be controlled, to be managed, to be monitored, to be checked, to get a grip (i.e. to control oneself)
conversar — to talk, converse.
convertir — to convert. Also: to turn, to transform, [with en ‘something’] to become, get, turn into, transform
convidar — to invite. Also: to offer
cooperante — volunteer worker, aid worker.
copiar — to copy, to crib. Also: to imitate
corazón — heart (organ of the body). Also: emotions, kindness, spirit, the center of an object, core (of a fruit), artichoke heart, heart (a playing card of the suit hearts, corazones)
cordero — lamb.
corona — crown. Also: crown (various units of currency), corona, wreath; ring, circle, sprocket; (bicycle sprockets) cassette, larger part of a pair of gear wheels, washer

cortar — to cut. Also: to cut off, cut out, cut through, cut down, cut up, to nip, to chop, chop up, chop off, to slice, to slit, to mow, to slash, to hack, to carve, to engrave, to hang up, to terminate a telephone call, to stop an action, to shut off, to finish a relationship, to haircut, to cut, to cut off, to slit (oneself or a part of the body)
corte — cut (the act of cutting). Also: cut (the result of cutting), cut (an opening resulting from cutting; an incision or wound), haircut, embarrassment, style or type, cross section
cortesía — courtesy. Also: politeness, comity
cotidiano — daily, quotidian.
creación — creation. Also: invention
crecer — to grow.
crimen — violent crime.
criticar — to criticize.

cruce — crossroads, crossing, intersection (a place where one road crosses another; an intersection of two or more roads). Also: crossing, (action of crossing or traversing (a room, street, sea, etc.), or time elapsed for it), crosswalk, crossing (action of causing to cross), crossbreeding (action, effect or product of producing (an organism) by the mating of individuals of different breeds, varieties, or species; hybridize)
cruz — cross. Also: torture; misery, tails (side of a coin)
crítico — critical (inclined to criticize), judgmental. Also: critical (pertaining to a crisis)

cuadro — square (a polygon with four straight sides of equal length and four right angles). Also: rectangle (any quadrilateral having opposing sides parallel and four right angles), picture, painting or other work of art, especially one in a frame, frame (a piece of photographic film containing an image), frame (a rigid, generally rectangular mounting for paper, canvas or other flexible material), table, chart, graph (a visual representation of data), sight, scene, picture, spectacle, image (an event that leaves an impact), group, team, squad; staff, frame (the structural element of a bicycle or motorcycle), panel, control panel (a flat surface onto which controls, instruments, and displays are mounted), leadership or management of an organization, ellipsis of cuadro clínico: symptom or symptoms, diamond, infield (the region of the field roughly bounded by the home plate, first base, second base and third base)

cuarto — fourth (in a series). Also: fourth, quarter (one of four equal divisions of a whole), quarter-hour (one of four equal divisions of an hour), watch (one of the four approximately equal divisions of the night, especially for shifts of watchmen), watch (one of the four equal divisions of soldiers, guards, police, &c. for routine patrols), English or American quart (a unit of liquid measure equal to ¹⁄₄ galón, equivalent to about 0.95 L), cuarto, quarter-sack (a traditional unit of dry measure equal to ¹⁄₄ saco, equivalent to about 27.8 L), quarter (one of the four main divisions of the body of quadrapeds and birds), quarter (one of the four main divisions of the body of criminals for public display), quarter (one of four main pieces of cloth used to tailor a garment), quarter (one of the four main phases of a heavenly body as it waxes and wanes in the sky), line, lineage (any of the four main lines of ancestry through one's various grandparents), quarters (various structures used for dwelling), synonym of sala (“room”), synonym of habitación (“bedroom”), synonym of piso (“apartment”), watch, shift (the time that a soldier, guard, police, etc. spends on patrol), lot (a piece of real estate, especially one of the divisions of a large piece of land being put to sale), service (particularly to a king or queen), well-proportioned limbs (especially in discussion of art and horses), cuarto (a former copper Spanish coin), synonym of dinero (“money”), synonym of dinero (“a fortune”)
cubo — cube (a regular polyhedron having six identical square faces). Also: cube (the third power of a number, value, term or expression)
cuello — neck (part of body connecting the head and the torso). Also: collar (part of a shirt fitting around the neck)
cuenca — eye socket. Also: watershed, catchment basin, valley
cuestión — matter, question, issue. Also: a question; a sentence that asks for information, thing
culebrón — soap opera. Also: large snake, crafty person
cultivar — to cultivate; to grow (plants, etc.).
culto — educated; cultured. Also: learned; scholarly, cultivated
cumbre — summit, peak, top, hilltop (topmost point or surface, especially of a mountain). Also: summit (gathering or assembly of leaders), apex, acme

curar — to care, heed, study, strive, seek, work. Also: to cure, heal (body, soul, mind, heart); nurse, treat, dress (a wound), to remedy (an evil), to clean, cleanse, to prepare, to cure, salt, smoke, to ripen, to dress, tan (animal hides), to season, dry (wood), to bleach (linen), to intoxicate, make drunk, to get drunk, to think, to laugh, to burst out laughing
currículum — curriculum vitae.
círculo — circle.
cóctel — cocktail. Also: cocktail party
cóndor — condor. Also: Andean condor
danza — dance.
decoración — decoration.
decorar — to learn by heart. Also: to recite, to syllabify
defecto — defect, fault, deficiency. Also: flaw, imperfection, default
delante — in front of, before (spatially). Also: forward, forwards (with hacia preceding it), ahead (with por preceding it)
delincuencia — crime, delinquency.
democrático — democratic (pertaining to democracy).
denominación — denomination.
dentista — dentist.
dentro — inside (for space). Also: indoors, within, in (for time)
depender — to depend, to be subject to. Also: to depend on, to rely on, to hinge on, to depend upon, to rely upon (+ de), to be up to (+ de)
dependiente — dependent.
deprimir — to depress. Also: to humiliate, to become depressed
derecha — right (direction). Also: right (ensemble of right-wing political parties; political conservatives as a group)
derrotar — to defeat.
desaparición — disappearance. Also: demise
desarrollar — to develop.
desarrollo — development.
desastre — disaster, catastrophe, calamity. Also: disaster, a debacle, a bust, mess, shambles, wreckage, something messed up, a hot mess, train wreck
descanso — rest, break. Also: relief, half-time
descender — to descend.

desconectar — to disconnect, to unplug, to disengage, to detach. Also: to switch off, to shut down, to turn off, to shut off, to unwind, to disconnect, to disengage, to relax (said of a person), to switch off (to lose interest, and start thinking about something else)
desconocido — unknown, undisclosed, unbeknownst. Also: unfamiliar, uncharted, undiscovered
describir — to describe, represent in words. Also: to trace, describe
descripción — description.
descubrimiento — discovery (something discovered). Also: discovery (the discovering of new things), unveiling
descuento — discount. Also: injury time, stoppage time (UK), additional time, added time
deseo — desire, wish.
desesperar — to despair, exasperate. Also: to despair (be driven to despair)
despacio — slowly. Also: quietly
despectivo — pejorative, derogatory; despective.
despierto — awake.
destinar — to appoint. Also: to set aside
destruir — to destroy.
detalle — detail. Also: nice gesture, beau geste, attention, small gift
determinar — to determine.
devolver — to return, refund, restore, give back, hand back, bring back, send back etc.. Also: to pay back, repay (to pay an amount of money owed to another), to devolve, to throw up, to vomit
diferencia — difference.
digno — worthy. Also: honorable, dignified, respectful
discreto — discrete. Also: discreet, tactful, modest, reasonable
disculpar — to excuse, forgive, pardon. Also: to apologize
discutir — to discuss, to argue; to talk about. Also: to examine some subject in detail
diseñar — to design.
disimular — to hide, to conceal. Also: to dissemble
dispensario — dispensary. Also: clinic
divino — divine. Also: heavenly
doble — double. Also: twofold
doler — to hurt (to cause physical or emotional pain). Also: to grieve, regret something; to feel sorry about, to complain (about)
domicilio — domicile. Also: residence, address
dominar — to dominate; to hold sway.
dormitorio — bedroom (a room in a house, apartment, hotel, or other dwelling where a bed is kept for sleeping). Also: dormitory
droga — drug. Also: indebtedness, debt
durar — to last. Also: to take time

repetir — to repeat (to do or say again (and again)). Also: to repeat; to take seconds (meal), to repeat oneself (to echo the words of (a person)), to repeat, to run, to rebroadcast (to broadcast again a television or radio program shown after), to leave an aftertaste, to burp
reportaje — report, article.
representación — representation.
representante — representative.
representar — to represent. Also: to make up, constitute, account for
republicano — republican. Also: Republican
república — republic.
requerir — to require, need. Also: to ask for, send for, call for
resfriado — a cold. Also: the flu
resistente — strong, tough, resistant. Also: heavy-duty
respetar — to respect.
respirar — to breathe.
responsabilidad — responsibility, responsibleness. Also: accountability, legal liability
resultar — to result, to turn out, to end up, to prove. Also: to work out, to seem, to look, to be pretty
resumir — to summarize.
retirar — to remove. Also: to withdraw, to retreat, to withdraw, to retire (for a reason other than age)
revisar — to revise. Also: to review, to check
revolucionario — revolutionary.
rico — rich (having a lot of money and possessions). Also: luxurious, tasty, yummy
rocío — dew (moisture on cool surfaces that condenses from the surrounding moist air). Also: sprinkle, shower (brief, light rain), quaff, chug, spray, ice plant (Mesembryanthemum crystallinum)

romano — Roman (of, from or relating to the city of Rome, capital of Italy). Also: Roman (of, from or relating to Rome (ancient empire covering much of the land surrounding the Mediterranean)), of Rome as the seat of power of the popes, heads of the Roman Catholic Church
romper — to break. Also: to break up, terminate (a relationship, friendship etc.), to begin to do something; to break into, to rupture
rosal — rosebush.
ruido — noise, racket, rattle, clatter, sound (various sounds, usually unwanted). Also: noise (unwanted part of a signal), uproar, talk, fuss
rumbo — course. Also: heading, bearing
récord — record (the most extreme known value of some achievement).
sabor — flavor; taste. Also: seasoning
salina — salt pan, salt marsh, salt works. Also: salt mine
salmón — salmon. Also: salmon (color)
salto — jump, leap, jumping. Also: skip, gap; difference, dive, jump, vault, waterfall
salvaje — wild, savage (untamed, not domesticated). Also: savage, feral, uncivilized (said of a person)
salvar — to save. Also: to rescue, to salvage, to cover (a distance)
sanidad — health.
sartén — frying pan.
secretario — secretary.
secuestrar — to kidnap, to abduct. Also: to hijack (of a vehicle), to sequester, seize, confiscate
secuestro — kidnapping.

seda — silk (fine fiber excreted by the silkworm or other arthropod). Also: silk (fine, soft cloth woven from silk fibers), thin string (long, very thin, and flexible structure made from threads twisted together)
sede — seat, headquarters. Also: venue, see, office
seguridad — safety, security. Also: self-confidence
seleccionar — to select.
selección — selection. Also: choice, national sports team
semestre — semester.
semilla — seed (of a plant).
sensación — sensation. Also: feeling
separación — separation.
serrano — sea bass. Also: Iberus gualtieranus, serrano (serrano chili)
sesión — session (meeting). Also: showing (in a cinema, theatre etc.), session (period of time), session (e.g. with a doctor, physio, psychologist), session
significado — significant. Also: important, well-known
signo — sign, indication. Also: clipping of signo zodiacal (“zodiac sign”)
sillón — armchair. Also: recliner
simbolizar — to symbolize, stand for.
sinónimo — synonym. Also: byword
sociedad — society, social order. Also: high society, community, company, firm, corporation, association, partnership
sofá — sofa, couch.
soledad — solitude.
solicitar — to request, to ask for, to solicit. Also: to attract
solidaridad — solidarity.
solidario — caring, sympathetic to; solidary.
solucionar — to solve; to implement a solution.

sombra — shadow (a dark image projected onto a surface where light (or other radiation) is blocked by the shade of an object). Also: shade (darkness where light, particularly sunlight, is blocked), shade, shadow (a small degree), ghost, shade, phantom, shade (a color obtained by adding black)
sombrero — hat. Also: butterfly pea (Clitorea ternatea)
soportar — to bear, to endure, to withstand, put up with, tolerate, to stomach, to weather, to handle. Also: to support a weight or load
sor — sister, female member of a religious community. Also: sister
soroche — altitude sickness, mountain sickness. Also: galena
sorprendente — surprising.
sostenible — sustainable.
subida — rise. Also: acclivity
suceder — to happen, to befall, to occur. Also: to succeed to, to follow
sueldo — salary. Also: any of several historical European units of currency, including the solidus, sol, and soldo
suicidar — to commit suicide. Also: to kill someone (and make their death appear to have been a suicide rather than a homicide)
suicidio — suicide (the act of killing oneself).

superar — to surpass, exceed. Also: to overcome, to beat, to outdo, to overtake, pass, to get over, to outdo each other, to one-up each other, to outdo oneself, to be overcome, to be exceeded, to be surmounted, to be broken (e.g. a record broken)
superficie — surface. Also: area (extension of land)
superior — upper, higher. Also: better, superior

suponer — to suppose, to surmise, to assume, to guess, to imagine, to figure, to say, to take it. Also: to presume, to expect, to comprise, make up, to pose (e.g. a risk, a threat, a danger), to mean, to imply, to represent, to involve, to entail, to hypothesize
surrealista — surreal. Also: surrealistic
suspender — to suspend. Also: to flunk, to fail to pass an exam or class
sí — yes; aye, ay; approbation, acceptance.
símbolo — symbol. Also: creed
taberna — pub, tavern.
tacón — heel (part of a shoe's sole). Also: high heel
taller — workshop. Also: studio (of an artist), repair shop, garage
tamaño — as big or small; equivalent in size; so big/so small; such a big/small.
tambor — drum. Also: drum-shaped piece, part or device, drum (wall that supports a dome)

tapar — to cover. Also: to cap, to close with a lid, to put a lid on, to plug, to plug up, to fill a hole or pit, to block, to obstruct, to clog, to cover up, to hide, to conceal, to mask (e.g., a smell, a painting or colors painted), to be clogged, to be clogged up, to cover oneself up (to cover oneself when naked), to cover oneself (to put on a covering for protection from the elements, etc.)
tardar — to delay. Also: to last, to be late
tarta — pie, tart. Also: cake, quiche
tatuaje — tattoo.
tatuar — to tattoo.
techo — ceiling. Also: roof
tecla — key, button (on a machine or instrument). Also: trigger; button (delicate topic)
tecnología — technology.
tecnológico — technological (of, relating to, or involving technology).
tejer — to knit. Also: to weave, to spin (a web, a tale)

tejido — texture (of a cloth). Also: textile (a cloth), weave (a type or way of weaving), weaving (a piece of woven material made on a loom), tissue (a group of cells similar in origin that function together to do a specific job)
telediario — newscast, TV news, (UK) TV news programme, (US) TV news program, TV news bulletin.
telefonear — to telephone.
telefónico — telephonic; pertaining to telephones.
televisor — television set, TV set, television receiver, television, (colloquial) telly (a device for receiving television signals and displaying them in visual form).
temblar — to tremble, to quiver, to quake, to shudder, to cringe, to quail. Also: to shake, to shiver, to twitch
temporada — season, period, span of time. Also: season, series (group of episodes of a television broadcast)
tendencia — tendency. Also: trend, fad
tensión — tension, tenseness. Also: stress, strain, voltage
teoría — theory.
terapia — therapy. Also: treatment
terreno — land, ground. Also: grounds (plural), pitch
tesis — thesis.

tierra — land, ground (the part of Earth which is not covered by oceans or other bodies of water). Also: earth, soil (the unconsolidated mineral or organic material on the immediate surface of the earth), land, terrain (an area of land or the particular features of it), land, terrain (reserved for growing crops), homeland (one's country of birth), land, country, nation, territory (a country or region), ground, earth (a connection electrically to the earth on equipment), earth (one of the four or five classical elements)
tigre — tiger. Also: jaguar, angry or fierce person, an energic or expert male lover, toilet, WC
timbre — bell. Also: doorbell, timbre, stamp, postmark, seal
todoterreno — all-terrain.
tono — tone.
toro — bull.
tostada — toast (toasted bread).
total — total, complete, outright.
traducir — to translate (from one language into another). Also: to translate, convert, turn (to, into).
tragedia — tragedy, catastrophe. Also: a tragedy play, sob story
tranquilidad — tranquility.
tranquilizar — to calm, calm down. Also: to sedate
transformar — to transform.
trasladar — to move. Also: to transfer, to translate, to copy, transcribe, to transmit, to move, change residence.
tratamiento — treatment (the process or manner of treating someone or something). Also: treatment (medical care for an illness or injury), term of address
trenza — plait, braid, tress, pigtail.
tribu — tribe.
trigo — wheat.
tristeza — sadness, gloom, sorrow, gloominess, unhappiness, misery. Also: tristeza (plant disease)

turno — turn (chance to use (something) shared in sequence with others). Also: turn (chance to make a move in a game), shift (set group of workers or period of working time), duty, guard, emergency service, appointment
tutear — to address someone informally with tú, rather than the formal usted or the informal pronoun vos used in certain countries; to thou.
té — tea.
técnico — technician. Also: face (a wrestler whose in-ring persona embodies heroic or virtuous traits)
túnel — tunnel. Also: nutmeg
urbanización — housing development. Also: housing estate, urbanization
urgencia — urgency. Also: emergency
uso — use. Also: usage; habit, wear (degradation)
uva — grape.
uña — fingernail, toenail, nail. Also: claw, hoof, stinger
vaca — cow (adult female of the species Bos taurus). Also: beef, leather, cow (woman considered unpleasant, particularly one considered fat), collection
vacación — vacation, holiday.
vacío — vacuum, void. Also: empty space; gap
valiente — brave, courageous, fearless. Also: valiant, gallant, doughty, gritty, plucky, gutsy, spunky

vaquero — cowherd, cowboy. Also: jeans, Ponte los vaqueros y trabaja., Ponte los vaqueros y trabaja. Put on the jeans and work., Put on the jeans and work., a kind of bird:, squirrel cuckoo (Piaya cayana), laughing falcon (Herpetotheres cachinnans), lesser ground cuckoo (Morococcyx erythropygus)
vasco — Basque.
vegano — vegan (relating to vegans or veganism).
vela — candle. Also: wakefulness, sleeplessness, vigil, watch, vigil (any of various indigenous festivals)
venezolano — a Venezuelan person. Also: venezolano (an obsolete currency of Venezuela)
verde — green (color/colour). Also: green (eco-friendly), unripe, inexperienced; naïve, risqué, naughty, dirty, coarse, unwell; sick
vergüenza — shame. Also: embarrassment
verso — verse, poem. Also: verse, line (of a poem), verso, reverse, back, overleaf (of a page), verso, left-hand page, small culverin (kind of cannon), lie, story, porky
vestido — dressed.
viceversa — vice versa, the other way round.
vicuña — vicuna (small, alpaca-like mammal).
vientre — abdomen. Also: belly, womb
vigilante — watchful, alert, wakeful.
vigilar — to watch over.
vinagre — vinegar (a sour liquid formed by the fermentation of alcohol used as a condiment or preservative).
visar — to study. Also: to visa, to endorse; to approve

visión — vision (something seen; an object perceived visually). Also: vision, sight (the sense or ability of sight), view (a way of understanding something, an opinion, a theory), vision (something imaginary one thinks one sees)
voluntario — voluntary.
válido — valid.
zafra — harvest, chiefly that of sugar cane. Also: sugar fabrication, season (part of the year when something happens)
zumo — juice (liquid from a plant). Also: extract; herbal tincture, profit; gain
ómnibus — public bus.

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
